_publications._publicationTitle: Quantitative MRI of dorsal root ganglion alterations in neurofibromatosis type 1 patients with or without pain.

_contextSnippet: The DRG T2 relaxation time in NF1 patients was longer than in healthy controls (median 118.2 versus 96.2 ms, p = 0.007; Fig. 2b). This was true for both L5 (median 107.4 versus 95.4 ms, p = 0.036) and S1 levels
details: Enabled quantitative T2 relaxometry showing increased DRG T2 relaxation time in NF1 patients (median 118.2 vs 96.2 ms, p = 0.007) at both L5 and S1 levels
